description: Five quality-of-life improvements: open channel policy, abort command, archive cleanup, scheduled tasks, and file-sending CLI tool.

userStories.title: Channel access policy — config, DB, and auto-register logic
userStories.description: As a gateway operator, I want to choose an open, open-trigger, or allowlist channel policy so that guild channels are automatically available without manual registration.
acceptanceCriteria: CHANNEL_POLICY env var accepted with values open, open-trigger, allowlist (default allowlist)
setup.ts wizard includes Channel Policy step
EXCLUDED_CHANNELS env var accepted as comma-separated channel IDs
discord.ts handleMessage auto-registers unregistered guild channels when policy is open or open-trigger
Auto-registered channels derive name from guild.name #channel.name, folder from ch_<id>, requiresTrigger from policy
userStories.notes: Completed. Config, discord.ts, setup.ts updated. 3 new tests.

userStories.title: Abort / cancel command
userStories.description: As a Discord user, I want a /pi stop slash command to abort the currently running pi invocation and clear queued messages.
acceptanceCriteria: queue.ts exports abortChannelTask(jid) that aborts active controller and clears pending messages
slash-commands.ts registers stop subcommand on /pi command
Active task aborted with confirmation message and cleared count
No active task case handled with informative message
Agent process receives SIGTERM via existing abort path
userStories.notes: Completed. abortChannelTask exported, /pi stop subcommand added, 1 new test.

userStories.title: Archived session auto-cleanup
userStories.description: As a gateway operator, I want archived sessions automatically deleted after a configurable retention period.
acceptanceCriteria: cleanupArchivedSessions() scans sessionsDir for __archived_<timestamp> dirs and deletes expired ones
Cleanup runs on 24-hour interval in gateway lifecycle
userStories.notes: Completed. archive-cleanup.ts, CLI archive list/cleanup, config, lifecycle, 7 tests.

userStories.title: Scheduled tasks — DB schema, scheduler engine, and CLI
userStories.description: As a pi agent, I want to create, list, enable, disable, and remove scheduled tasks via CLI so the gateway executes prompts on schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: scheduled_tasks table with id, name, type, schedule, channel_jid, prompt, enabled, last_run_at, next_run_at, created_at, created_by
DB functions: addTask, removeTask, enableTask, disableTask, listTasks, updateTaskAfterRun
scheduler.ts checks every 30s for due tasks and enqueues them
CLI commands: task add, task list, task remove, task enable, task disable
userStories.notes: Completed. scheduled_tasks DB, scheduler.ts, task CLI, croner, tests.

userStories.title: Send-file tool — CLI command for pi to send files to Discord
userStories.description: As a pi agent, I want a CLI command to send files with optional text to a Discord channel.
acceptanceCriteria: Uses Discord REST API directly with bot token from config (no IPC needed)
File size limits respected (MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES, max 10 files)
userStories.notes: Completed. direct send CLI, send.ts, validation, tests.
